title: 1 2 Chain Rule
body:
1.2 THE CHAIN RULE

            The Chain Rule for functions of more than one variable:
                       If z = f (x, y) and x = x(t) and y = y(t) then

                                   ∂z   ∂z ∂x ∂z ∂y
                                      =       +
                                   ∂t   ∂x ∂t   ∂y ∂t

                    If z = f (x, y) and x = x(u, v) and y = y(u, v) then

                  ∂z   ∂z ∂x ∂z ∂y                   ∂z   ∂z ∂x ∂z ∂y
                     =      +               and         =      +
                  ∂u   ∂x ∂u ∂y ∂u                   ∂v   ∂x ∂v ∂y ∂v


A common situation is that z is a function of x and y with x and y themselves functions
of other variables, say u and v. Then z is also a function of u and v and it makes sense
                              ∂z
to ask the question “what is     ?”
                              ∂u
The chain rule enables us to answer this question without actually ever having to calculate
z as an explicit function of u and v. It is usually much more effective to use the chain
rule than to calculate the new function w(u, v) = z (x(u, v), y(u, v)).
Two version of the chain rule are shown above. However, all you need to remember
is that you just apply the single-variable chain rule to each argument of z.
                                                                                       dz
Example 1 Given z = x2 y 3 with x = 2t and y = sin(4t), use the chain rule to find        .
                                                                                       dt
Verify your answer by first substituting x(t) and y(t) into z(x, y).

                                             F 8t sin3 (4t) + 48t2 sin2 (4t) cos(4t) F


                                                                         ∂z     ∂z

 Example 5 Suppose that temperature in the plane is given by T (x, y) = x2 +y 2 . A par-
ticle is traveling along a path C defined parametrically by x = t − 1 y = t3 − 3t2 + 3t + 1
where t is time in seconds. Show that T (t) has a stationary point when t = 1.




Example 6 If w = f (u, v) with u = x + y and v = x − y show that
